title: salix aurita subsp. uliginosa
intro: Salix aurita subsp. uliginosa, also known as the bog willow or marsh willow, is a subspecies of the Salix aurita plant, a type of willow tree native to Europe and Asia. The bark of Salix aurita subsp. uliginosa contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The medicinal properties of this plant make it beneficial for relieving headaches, fever, and rheumatic pain, as well as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Salix aurita subsp. uliginosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including menstrual cramps and digestive issues.
